name: Mobile Patterns
title: Native Mobile Development Patterns
description: Production-grade patterns for Native iOS (Swift/SwiftUI) and Android (Kotlin/Jetpack Compose) development, focusing on MVVM, clean architecture, and performance.

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: Patterns are based on official Apple and Google documentation and community best practices.
axiomAlignment.A2_user_primacy: Focuses on delivering high-quality, responsive user experiences.
axiomAlignment.A3_transparency: Explicitly defines architectural layers and dependency management.
axiomAlignment.A4_non_harm: Prioritizes secure data storage and efficient resource usage to prevent device strain.
axiomAlignment.A5_consistency: Enforces unified architectural patterns across both mobile platforms.

MVVM-Reactive.description: Standard architecture for reactive UI updates in native apps using Combine (iOS) or StateFlow (Android).
MVVM-Reactive.use_when: Building modern interactive mobile UIs that require real-time state synchronization.
best_practices: Use reactive streams for all UI-binding logic.
Ensure state updates occur on the main thread.

Coordinator-Pattern.use_when: Managing complex navigation flows in large mobile applications.
best_practices: Each coordinator should represent a single flow.
Inject dependencies into coordinators to maintain decoupling.
Store a reference to child coordinators to prevent premature deallocation.
Always use background threads for network and disk operations.
Implement accessibility identifiers for all interactive elements to support automated testing.
Use localized strings and assets from day one.
Handle low-memory and backgrounding states gracefully.

anti_patterns.name: Massive View Controller
anti_patterns.problem: Putting complex business logic and networking inside UI classes leads to unmaintainable code.
anti_patterns.fix: Deconstruct into ViewModels, Coordinators, and Services.
